brandVoice.text: Direct, developer-first clarity. Stripe speaks like an expert who respects the reader's intelligence — no filler, no marketing fluff. Technical terms are preserved exactly.

instructions.name: developer-mode
instructions.text: Address developers directly using 'du' (informal you). Never use 'Sie'.

instructions.name: code-preservation
instructions.text: All code snippets, class names, parameter names, and CLI commands must remain in English exactly as written.

instructions.name: technical-nouns
instructions.text: Product names (Dashboard, Connect, Radar, Terminal) are never translated.

scorers.name: du-form-check
scorers.instruction: Verify that informal 'du' is used consistently, not 'Sie'.

scorers.name: code-integrity
scorers.instruction: All inline code and API references match the English source exactly.

brandVoice.text: Minimal and precise. Linear values speed and clarity above all else. Language is trimmed to its essentials — active voice, short sentences, no redundancy.

instructions.name: brevity-rule
instructions.text: Omit articles and filler words wherever grammatically acceptable in German. Prefer shorter phrasing.

instructions.name: active-voice
instructions.text: Always prefer active voice. Passive constructions should only be used if there is no shorter active alternative.

scorers.name: sentence-length
scorers.instruction: Score based on average words per sentence — shorter is better. Target under 15 words/sentence.

brandVoice.text: Aspirational and empowering. Vercel speaks to builders with optimism — every sentence suggests that building fast, beautiful things is within reach. Modern and forward-looking.

instructions.name: optimism-tone
instructions.text: Frame all capabilities positively. Avoid negations — 'you can now...' not 'you no longer have to...'.

instructions.name: du-form
instructions.text: Use informal 'du' to address users, matching the platform's developer-friendly tone.

scorers.name: positive-framing
scorers.instruction: Check that no paragraph opens with a negation or a problem statement without an immediate solution.

brandVoice.text: Warm, accessible, and human. Notion speaks like a thoughtful colleague, not a tech product. It balances friendliness with competence, using everyday language that makes powerful features feel approachable.

instructions.name: approachability
instructions.text: Avoid jargon. If a technical term must be used, follow it with a brief plain-language explanation in parentheses on first mention.

instructions.name: du-form
instructions.text: Use informal 'du' throughout. The Notion brand is casual and personable.

instructions.name: feature-names
instructions.text: UI element names and feature names (Board, Timeline, Gallery) are kept in English.

scorers.name: jargon-free
scorers.instruction: Flag any untranslated technical English term that is not in the non-translatables list.

scorers.name: reading-ease
scorers.instruction: Estimate readability for a non-technical German audience. Score 0-100 where 100 is most readable.
